text: This will be a discovery research interview and the questions will be open ended.
There are no right or wrong answers.
So answer as honestly as you can.

I think for me it is the different studies that are available.
So looking at the short studies or the longer studies and just the titles, I think more than anything to see if the titles were something that stood out to me.
Once you see a title that stands out, how do you decide whether or not to actually click on it?
For me I tend to click on all of them, the studies that actually stand out to me because like when you click on it you can get some more information to see if it really is relevant or not.
But sometimes it'll be things like what the, what type of interview it is, if it's longer, what the financial return is as well, and how long it will take us too.
They're all quite relevant.

Oh yes, I should be able to.
There have been a couple around banking where I think, oh yes, that's something that is going to be relevant to me.

But then you actually go into it and it's almost like it actually the, the topic that they're looking at is more at banking from business perspective or people that are involved in finances for a business rather than consumer or personal finances.
And it's not clear, clear from the title itself what it's leading into.
When you realize a banking study is for business owners rather than consumers, at what point in the application process does that usually become clear to you?
It's usually at the point where I'm actually answering the questions themselves.
So you might like say, yep, I'm available at X, Y and Z times, happy for it to be recorded, et cetera, et cetera.
And then it starts to go, okay, for what business?
Like what kind of business do you work for?
What's your position in the business?
And you start to realize that like these aren't questions that I would expect to be asked for like a screening for like a consumer banking situation.
And then you know, you, you answer those questions and then it says what's your decision making role in your business?
And then it's like at that point I go okay, yep, no, not relevant for me.
That sounds like quite a few steps to go through.
Just to find out it's not a match.
How does it affect your.
How does having to go through those screening questions before finding out it's not a match affect your experience with the app?
It certainly makes me feel a bit frustrated.
I feel like there is definitely a chance for them to put that information earlier.
Like when you click into the particular option or experience itself and it provides a bit of a blurb.
I think that's a perfect opportunity to provide that bit of information rather than making people have to actually go through the screening questions.
So it's just a bit frustrating.
Thank you for sharing those insights.
